title: Week 3 Day 2 (2/2) — Pipeline tasks
summary: Part 2 of 2 · Sentiment, NER, Q&A, summarization, translation, zero-shot, text-gen.

sections.heading: Sentiment analysis
sections.remember: `pipeline("sentiment-analysis", device="cuda")` — default model may misread sarcasm; swap **`model=`** from Hub.
points: Example: positive label ~99.9% on clear praise.
"I should be more excited" → default may still say positive; try **star-rating** models (e.g. BERT-based).

sections.heading: Named entity recognition (NER)
points: Use case: extract product names from chat → lookup in DB (baby step toward **RAG**).

sections.heading: Question answering
sections.remember: Needs **question + context** — model finds answer span in context (not full RAG retrieval).
points: Pattern: NER on question → fetch context from DB → QA pipeline.

sections.heading: Summarization & translation
sections.remember: Small specialized models can summarize/translate **cheaply** vs one big frontier API — you own weights + code.
points: `translation_en_to_fr` (or other pairs via Hub model id).
Hub has filters for language and task.

sections.heading: Zero-shot classification
sections.remember: **Zero-shot** = no training examples — you supply **label buckets** and the model scores each.

sections.heading: Text generation (GPT-2)
sections.remember: Default `text-generation` often uses **GPT-2** — old, **plausible but not truthful**; shows next-token imitation.
points: Contrast with frontier models: quality comes from scale + training, not magic architecture alone.
Experiment: change `model=` on Hub for better open models.

cheatSheet: Tasks: sentiment · ner · question-answering · summarization · translation · zero-shot-classification · text-generation
Always try different Hub model= ids
Small task models vs one GPT — tradeoff table
